medical_history.chief_complaint: A 70-year-old woman presented to our emergency department with a severe left-sided headache.
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A 70-year-old woman presented to the Carle Emergency Department with a 2-week history of intractable left-sided headache. She described her headache as a throbbing, sharp pain that begins above her left eye and radiates to the left parietal area. She came to the hospital owing to the headache’s worsening severity refractory to acetaminophen. Associated symptoms included photophobia, lacrimation, positional dizziness, and occasional ear pain.
medical_history.past_medical_history: She was a former smoker with a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, hypertension, myocardial infarction, left bundle branch block, and implantable cardioverter-defibrillator in place. She did not have a history of headaches.

physical_examination.general_examination: On physical exam, some fullness was noted in the left neck, with no carotid bruits bilaterally.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: A computed tomography (CT) angiogram with contrast revealed an avidly enhancing mass within the left carotid sheath measuring 10 mm × 15 mm × 14 mm. The mass splayed the left proximal internal and external carotid arteries. Serum and urine metanephrine results were negative. These findings were consistent with a nonfunctional CBT, preoperatively classified as Shamblin type I.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Avidly enhancing mass within the left carotid sheath measuring 10 mm × 15 mm × 14 mm. The mass splayed the left proximal internal and external carotid arteries.

other_tests.findings: Some fullness was noted in the left neck, with no carotid bruits bilaterally.
Confirmed the benign nature of her carotid body paraganglioma.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with a severe left-sided headache, photophobia, and lacrimation, initially consistent with paroxysmal hemicrania. A CT angiogram revealed a left carotid body tumor, which was nonfunctional based on negative serum and urine metanephrine results. The resolution of headache symptoms post-surgical resection of the tumor supported the diagnosis of the carotid body tumor as the cause of the symptoms.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: surgical resection of the carotid body tumor
